text.title: python-poetry-setup Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for python-poetry-setup as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent calls poetry shell in headless CI or Docker.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: poetry shell hangs forever waiting for a TTY. Poetry 2.0 removed it entirely. Always use poetry run <cmd> from agents, never poetry shell.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses pip install to add a package to a Poetry-managed project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: pip install bypasses Poetry's solver and does not update pyproject.toml or poetry.lock. The next poetry install will remove the package.

failure-mode.description: Agent writes "^0.1" expecting it to mean <1.0.0.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In Poetry, ^0.1 means >=0.1.0,<0.2.0 (not <1.0.0). The caret rule applies to the leftmost non-zero digit. Always run poetry add --dry-run and read back the resolved range before committing.

failure-mode.description: Agent calls poetry add X when the lockfile is dirty (uncommitted local changes).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Poetry silently merges the dirty state into the new lock. Run poetry check --lock before any add/remove.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses poetry self add instead of pip install to install a Poetry plugin.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Actually reversed: pip install into the Poetry interpreter corrupts Poetry's own deps. Use poetry self add for plugins. The antipattern is using pip for Poetry's own environment.
